categories.name: أَشْيَاءُ مِنْ قِصَّةِ يُوسُفَ عَلَيْهِ السَّلَام
categories.nameEn: Items from the story of Prophet Yusuf (AS)

items.verse: قَالَ قَآئِلٌۭ مِّنْهُمْ لَا تَقْتُلُوا۟ يُوسُفَ وَأَلْقُوهُ فِى غَيَـٰبَتِ ٱلْجُبِّ يَلْتَقِطْهُ بَعْضُ ٱلسَّيَّارَةِ إِن كُنتُمْ فَـٰعِلِينَ
items.verseEn: Said a speaker among them, "Do not kill Joseph but throw him into the bottom of the well; some travelers will pick him up - if you would do [something]."

items.verse: قَالَ إِنِّى لَيَحْزُنُنِىٓ أَن تَذْهَبُوا۟ بِهِۦ وَأَخَافُ أَن يَأْكُلَهُ ٱلذِّئْبُ وَأَنتُمْ عَنْهُ غَـٰفِلُونَ
items.verseEn: [Jacob] said, "Indeed, it saddens me that you should take him, and I fear that a wolf would eat him while you are of him unaware."

items.verse: قَالُوا۟ نَفْقِدُ صُوَاعَ ٱلْمَلِكِ وَلِمَن جَآءَ بِهِۦ حِمْلُ بَعِيرٍۢ وَأَنَا۠ بِهِۦ زَعِيمٌۭ
items.verseEn: They said, "We are missing the measure of the king. And for he who produces it is [the reward of] a camel's load, and I am responsible for it."

items.verse: وَقَالَ ٱلْمَلِكُ إِنِّىٓ أَرَىٰ سَبْعَ بَقَرَٰتٍۢ سِمَانٍۢ يَأْكُلُهُنَّ سَبْعٌ عِجَافٌۭ وَسَبْعَ سُنۢبُلَـٰتٍ خُضْرٍۢ وَأُخَرَ يَابِسَـٰتٍۢ ۖ يَـٰٓأَيُّهَا ٱلْمَلَأُ أَفْتُونِى فِى رُءْيَـٰىَ إِن كُنتُمْ لِلرُّءْيَا تَعْبُرُونَ
items.verseEn: And [subsequently] the king said, "Indeed, I have seen [in a dream] seven fat cows being eaten by seven [that were] lean, and seven green spikes [of grain] and others [that were] dry. O eminent ones, explain to me my vision, if you should interpret visions."

categories.name: مظاهر العفو والرحمة الإلهية
categories.nameEn: Expressions of Divine Forgiveness and Mercy

items.verse: أُو۟لَـٰٓئِكَ جَزَآؤُهُم مَّغْفِرَةٌۭ مِّن رَّبِّهِمْ وَجَنَّـٰتٌۭ تَجْرِى مِن تَحْتِهَا ٱلْأَنْهَـٰرُ خَـٰلِدِينَ فِيهَا ۚ وَنِعْمَ أَجْرُ ٱلْعَـٰمِلِينَ
items.verseEn: Those - their reward is forgiveness from their Lord and gardens beneath which rivers flow [in Paradise], wherein they will abide eternally; and excellent is the reward of the [righteous] workers.

items.verse: وَلَمَّا جَآءَ أَمْرُنَا نَجَّيْنَا هُودًۭا وَٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ مَعَهُۥ بِرَحْمَةٍۢ مِّنَّا وَنَجَّيْنَـٰهُم مِّنْ عَذَابٍ غَلِيظٍۢ
items.verseEn: And when Our command came, We saved Hūd and those who believed with him, by mercy from Us; and We saved them from a harsh punishment.

items.verse: أَلَمْ يَعْلَمُوٓا۟ أَنَّ ٱللَّهَ هُوَ يَقْبَلُ ٱلتَّوْبَةَ عَنْ عِبَادِهِۦ وَيَأْخُذُ ٱلصَّدَقَـٰتِ وَأَنَّ ٱللَّهَ هُوَ ٱلتَّوَّابُ ٱلرَّحِيمُ
items.verseEn: Do they not know that it is Allāh who accepts repentance from His servants and receives charities and that it is Allāh who is the Accepting of Repentance, the Merciful?

items.verse: إِن تُبْدُوا۟ خَيْرًا أَوْ تُخْفُوهُ أَوْ تَعْفُوا۟ عَن سُوٓءٍۢ فَإِنَّ ٱللَّهَ كَانَ عَفُوًّۭا قَدِيرًا
items.verseEn: If [instead] you show [some] good or conceal it or pardon an offense - indeed, Allāh is ever Pardoning and Competent.

categories.name: أَفْعَالُ الْخَلْقِ وَالْإِنْبَاتِ
categories.nameEn: Divine Actions of Bringing Forth or Causing to Grow

items.verse: قُلْ مَن يَرْزُقُكُم مِّنَ ٱلسَّمَآءِ وَٱلْأَرْضِ أَمَّن يَمْلِكُ ٱلسَّمْعَ وَٱلْأَبْصَـٰرَ وَمَن يُخْرِجُ ٱلْحَىَّ مِنَ ٱلْمَيِّتِ وَيُخْرِجُ ٱلْمَيِّتَ مِنَ ٱلْحَىِّ وَمَن يُدَبِّرُ ٱلْأَمْرَ ۚ فَسَيَقُولُونَ ٱللَّهُ ۚ فَقُلْ أَفَلَا تَتَّقُونَ
items.verseEn: Say, "Who provides for you from the heaven and the earth? Or who controls hearing and sight and who brings the living out of the dead and brings the dead out of the living and who arranges [every] matter?" They will say, "Allāh," so say, "Then will you not fear Him?"
And Allāh has caused you to grow from the earth a [progressive] growth.

items.verse: وَجَعَلْنَا فِيهَا جَنَّـٰتٍۢ مِّن نَّخِيلٍۢ وَأَعْنَـٰبٍۢ وَفَجَّرْنَا فِيهَا مِنَ ٱلْعُيُونِ
items.verseEn: And We placed therein gardens of palm trees and grapevines and caused to burst forth therefrom some springs -
Then We broke open the earth, splitting [it with sprouts],

categories.name: أَصْوَاتُ الْأَحْدَاثِ الْعَظِيمَةِ
categories.nameEn: Sounds of Momentous Events (Divine Decree, Judgment, or Significant Occurrences)

items.verseEn: So the shriek seized them at sunrise.

items.verse: وَنُفِخَ فِى ٱلصُّورِ فَإِذَا هُم مِّنَ ٱلْأَجْدَاثِ إِلَىٰ رَبِّهِمْ يَنسِلُونَ
items.verseEn: And the Horn will be blown; and at once from the graves to their Lord they will hasten.

items.verse: لَا يَسْمَعُونَ حَسِيسَهَا ۖ وَهُمْ فِى مَا ٱشْتَهَتْ أَنفُسُهُمْ خَـٰلِدُونَ
items.verseEn: They will not hear its sound, while they are, in that which their souls desire, abiding eternally.

items.verse: فَأَقْبَلَتِ ٱمْرَأَتُهُۥ فِى صَرَّةٍۢ فَصَكَّتْ وَجْهَهَا وَقَالَتْ عَجُوزٌ عَقِيمٌۭ
items.verseEn: And his wife approached with a cry [of alarm] and struck her face and said, "[I am] a barren old woman!"

deduction.title: The Voice of the Unseen
deduction.intro: In the annals of divine communication, a figure stands out whose story is woven with miraculous signs and steadfast faith. This individual received a unique revelation that challenged the norms of their time, speaking from beyond the veil of ordinary perception. Their message came not through conventional means but through a profound connection that defied human understanding, calling people to recognize the truth in unexpected ways. The journey of this servant of Allah serves as a testament to how guidance can manifest in the most extraordinary circumstances, inviting reflection on the nature of revelation itself.
clues: I was given a message that came through a mysterious voice from an unseen source.
My people initially rejected my message, demanding more conventional proof of my truthfulness.
I challenged them to produce something similar to what I had received, but they could not.
The revelation I brought was described as clear verses that made the truth evident.
I warned my people of severe punishment if they continued in their disbelief and arrogance.
My story is mentioned alongside other messengers in a surah that emphasizes the signs of Allah's power.

trial.label: Trial/Event
options: Being thrown into a fiery furnace
Producing a she-camel as a miraculous sign
Being cast into the sea in a chest
trial.answer: Producing a she-camel as a miraculous sign

outcome.label: Outcome
outcome.answer: My people were destroyed for killing the miraculous she-camel
options: My people repented and were saved
I was taken up to heaven alive
My people were destroyed for killing the miraculous she-camel
I was rescued by being put in an ark

deduction.arabic: مَآ أَنتَ إِلَّا بَشَرٌۭ مِّثْلُنَا فَأْتِ بِـَٔايَةٍ إِن كُنتَ مِنَ ٱلصَّـٰدِقِينَ
deduction.verse: You are but a man like ourselves, so bring a sign, if you should be of the truthful." (26:154)

translations: And worship Allah And (do) not associate with Him anything and to the parents (do) good
and with the relatives and the orphans and the needy and the neighbor (who is) near and the neighbor
(who is) farther away and the companion by your side and the traveler and what possess[ed] your right hands
Indeed Allah (does) not love (the one) who is [a] proud (and) [a] boastful
scramble.hint: This verse emphasizes the importance of monotheism and social responsibilities, including kindness to family and vulnerable groups.
scramble.arabic: ۞ وَٱعْبُدُوا۟ ٱللَّهَ وَلَا تُشْرِكُوا۟ بِهِۦ شَيْـًۭٔا ۖ وَبِٱلْوَٰلِدَيْنِ إِحْسَـٰنًۭا وَبِذِى ٱلْقُرْبَىٰ وَٱلْيَتَـٰمَىٰ وَٱلْمَسَـٰكِينِ وَٱلْجَارِ ذِى ٱلْقُرْبَىٰ وَٱلْجَارِ ٱلْجُنُبِ وَٱلصَّاحِبِ بِٱلْجَنۢبِ وَٱبْنِ ٱلسَّبِيلِ وَمَا مَلَكَتْ أَيْمَـٰنُكُمْ ۗ إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ لَا يُحِبُّ مَن كَانَ مُخْتَالًۭا فَخُورًا
words: ۞ وَٱعْبُدُوا۟ ٱللَّهَ وَلَا تُشْرِكُوا۟ بِهِۦ شَيْـًۭٔا ۖ وَبِٱلْوَٰلِدَيْنِ إِحْسَـٰنًۭا
وَبِذِى ٱلْقُرْبَىٰ وَٱلْيَتَـٰمَىٰ وَٱلْمَسَـٰكِينِ وَٱلْجَارِ ذِى ٱلْقُرْبَىٰ وَٱلْجَارِ
ٱلْجُنُبِ وَٱلصَّاحِبِ بِٱلْجَنۢبِ وَٱبْنِ ٱلسَّبِيلِ وَمَا مَلَكَتْ أَيْمَـٰنُكُمْ ۗ
إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ لَا يُحِبُّ مَن كَانَ مُخْتَالًۭا فَخُورًا
scramble.verseEn: Worship Allāh and associate nothing with Him, and to parents do good, and to relatives, orphans, the needy, the near neighbor, the neighbor farther away, the companion at your side, the traveler, and those whom your right hands possess. Indeed, Allāh does not like those who are self-deluding and boastful,

verse.arabic_text: إِنَّ اللَّهَ اشْتَرَىٰ مِنَ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ أَنْفُسَهُمْ وَأَمْوَالَهُمْ بِأَنَّ لَهُمُ الْجَنَّةَ ۚ يُقَاتِلُونَ فِي سَبِيلِ اللَّهِ فَيَقْتُلُونَ وَيُقْتَلُونَ ۖ وَعْدًا عَلَيْهِ حَقًّا فِي التَّوْرَاةِ وَالْإِنْجِيلِ وَالْقُرْآنِ ۚ وَمَنْ أَوْفَىٰ بِعَهْدِهِ مِنَ اللَّهِ ۚ فَاسْتَبْشِرُوا بِبَيْعِكُمُ الَّذِي بَايَعْتُمْ بِهِ ۚ وَذَٰلِكَ هُوَ الْفَوْزُ الْعَظِيمُ
verse.translation: Indeed, Allah has purchased from the believers their lives and their properties [in exchange] for that they will have Paradise. They fight in the cause of Allah, so they kill and are killed. [It is] a true promise [binding] upon Him in the Torah and the Gospel and the Qur'an. And who is truer to his covenant than Allah? So rejoice in your transaction which you have contracted. And it is that which is the great attainment.

theme_question.correct: The divine transaction: Allah's purchase of believers' lives and wealth in exchange for Paradise
options: The importance of charity and zakat in Islam
The divine transaction: Allah's purchase of believers' lives and wealth in exchange for Paradise
The prohibition of usury and interest in financial dealings
The virtues of patience during times of hardship

educational_notes.verse_context: This verse from Surah At-Tawbah (9:111) comes in the context of discussing the rewards for those who strive in the cause of Allah. It follows verses describing the believers who responded to Allah's call and the Prophet's mission. The verse presents a powerful metaphor of a divine transaction where Allah 'purchases' the believers' lives and properties, offering Paradise in return. This concept is particularly significant as it appears in the only surah of the Quran that doesn't begin with 'Bismillah' and deals extensively with themes of warfare, treaties, and the relationship between Muslims and non-believers.
educational_notes.theme_explanation: The verse introduces the profound concept of a 'transaction' (bay') between Allah and the believers. Unlike worldly transactions, this divine exchange involves the ultimate sacrifice (life and wealth) for the ultimate reward (Paradise). The theme emphasizes that true success comes from complete submission to Allah's cause, even at the cost of one's life. It validates this promise by noting its presence in all three Abrahamic scriptures (Torah, Gospel, and Quran), highlighting the consistency of divine promise across revelations. The theme encourages believers to find joy and assurance in this eternal covenant.
educational_notes.surah_overview: Surah At-Tawbah (The Repentance) is the 9th surah of the Quran and the only one that doesn't begin with 'Bismillah'. It's a Madinan surah consisting of 129 verses. The surah deals with several important themes: the termination of treaties with polytheists, regulations concerning warfare, the importance of jihad, criticism of hypocrites, and the obligation of zakat. It's named 'At-Tawbah' (Repentance) due to its emphasis on Allah's acceptance of repentance. The surah contains stern warnings to those who break covenants and strong encouragement for believers to remain steadfast. Juz 11 contains the latter part of this surah (from verse 93 onward) along with the beginning of Surah Yunus and Surah Hud.

harf.hint: A natural phenomenon mentioned in the Quran that brings rain and shade
harf.arabicVerse: هُوَ ٱلَّذِى يُرِيكُمُ ٱلْبَرْقَ خَوْفًۭا وَطَمَعًۭا وَيُنشِئُ ٱلسَّحَابَ ٱلثِّقَالَ
harf.verse: 13:12 — It is He who shows you lightning, [causing] fear and aspiration, and generates the heavy clouds.
